title: Elegant Twin Bed with Detailed Headboard
summary: A tall, crown-moulded headboard and matching footboard on a simple 2x4 and plywood frame — the moulding is what elevates a plain panel bed.
description:
Underneath the moulding, this is a straightforward panel bed: 2x4 legs, a plywood panel front, and a top rail on the headboard; a mitered 1x4 frame around a plywood panel on the footboard. What makes it read as "elegant" rather than utilitarian is entirely in the trim — crown moulding around the top of the headboard, base moulding applied to both panels — so take real care with the moulding miters. A slightly gapped miter joint is the single most obvious sign of a rushed build on a piece like this.

This headboard is tall and the bed will get pushed against and climbed into regularly, so anchor it to a wall stud behind the bed, not just to the side rails. A headboard this size relying only on the side-rail connection for stability is a genuine tip-forward risk, not just a suggestion.

tools.note: Crown and base moulding miters need to be tight — take the extra time here.

materials.name: 1x12 board, 8 ft
materials.note: Side rails — cut to 75" for a twin mattress.

steps.title: Build the headboard frame
steps.body: Attach the headboard top rail to the two legs, then attach the two 1x4 front trim boards flush to the front face.

steps.title: Attach the headboard panel and header
steps.body: Attach the plywood panel to the legs, top rail, and 1x4 trim, screwing into all of them for a solid attachment. Then attach the header board to the top of the headboard frame.

steps.title: Add crown moulding to the headboard
steps.body: Miter and attach crown moulding around the top of the headboard. Cut a test miter in scrap first and check the fit before committing your real moulding — crown moulding miters read the corner angle differently than flat trim, and it's the detail people notice first on a piece like this.

steps.title: Build the footboard frame
steps.body: Build the footboard frame from the 1x3 bottom and 1x4 sides and top/bottom rails, using 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws throughout.

steps.title: Attach the footboard panel and base moulding
steps.body: Attach the plywood panel to the back of the footboard frame. Apply base moulding to the bottom and around the inside edge of the panel, nailing and gluing it down — the moulding is what turns a plain panel into a detailed one, so keep your miters tight here too.

steps.title: Add side rails, cleats, and slats
steps.body:
Attach cleats to the inside faces of both side rails at a height matched to your mattress or box spring. Attach the side rails to the headboard and footboard, then rest slats (or a box spring) across the cleats — cut slats from 1x or 2x stock to the 39" inside width if not using a box spring.

Given the height and weight of this headboard, anchor it to a wall stud behind the bed before loading it up — don't rely on the side-rail connection alone to keep it from tipping forward.

images.alt: Twin bed with a tall crown-moulded headboard and matching footboard
